_comment: Cookie — Halfling Ankuspawn Dancer. Starter campaign levels 1-10. Generated by generate_starter_campaign.py, creative fill by DM.

player_input.name: Cookie
player_input.class_concept: Dancer (bard/healer — buffs via dancing and singing, high charm, support)
player_input.background: Ankuspawn raised by single halfling mother in a coalition town near Varenholm. Spent youth cleaning inns. Saved 5 GP over years for bard university tuition. Mom forbids the bard life — 'no daughter of mine will grow up to be a slut.' Today is her 21st birthday. She has to break the news.
player_input.personal_goal: Become a famous bard like Ignis

appearance.height_build: 4'10", athletic dancer's build — curvy but toned from years of dancing, compact halfling frame with surprising flexibility
appearance.hair: Bright blue, worn in twin tails — bouncy and expressive, moves with her when she dances
appearance.eyes: Gold-flecked (Ankuspawn trait) — warm golden glow against dark skin, catches firelight
appearance.distinguishing_marks: Faint golden scroll-like markings on shoulders and torso — she thinks they're birthmarks, actually Ember traces from her Ankuspawn heritage. Pointed ears (halfling, slightly elongated from Ankuspawn blood). Small gold hoop earrings.
appearance.style_vibe: Bold and flashy — favors deep blues, reds, and golds. Dancer's clothes: flowing skirts, lace trim, ribbon accents, bare feet or light dancing shoes. Moves like she's always mid-performance. Confident smile that makes people underestimate how sharp she is. The kind of presence that fills a room the moment she walks in.

player_knowledge: Cookie knows she has gold-flecked eyes. Her mom never explained why.
Cookie knows the Dragon Emperor exists — everyone does. She does not know he is her father.
Cookie knows Ignis as a famous bard — 'The Firebird' — who performs kingdom-wide with magical fire shows. Ignis is her idol and the reason she wants to be a bard.
Cookie has heard of the Ashenveil Academy — a prestigious school in the southeast. She does NOT know about the Cult of Anku or Ankuspawn harvesting.
Cookie has felt strange things — emotions from strangers, a warmth in her chest when she dances, men staring too long. She thinks she's just pretty. She doesn't know it's an aura.
Cookie's mom never talks about her father. 'He was nobody. Forget it.'
dm_only_crossover_hooks: Cookie's mother met Kenji at an inn 22 years ago. He was passing through — Bane of Eve encounter. Cookie's mom never learned his real name.
The Cult of Anku has agents in Varenholm looking for Ankuspawn. A female Ankuspawn with gold eyes enrolling in bard school will eventually appear on their radar.
Ignis occasionally performs at the Varenholm Academy during festival season. Cookie could meet her idol — and not know they share a father.
Vess (Kenji's former companion) lives in the Varenholm area and is desperate for Nyx's fertility elixir. She might cross paths with Cookie without either knowing the connection.
inherited_lore.lore_seed: [Cult of Anku — Ankuspawn as targets] A young woman with gold eyes disappeared from the next town over three months ago. Her mother received a letter saying she'd been 'accepted to a special program at the Ashenveil.' She never wrote again. (Connects to: Nyx's Ankuspawn harvesting operation — Cookie is exactly the kind of person they're looking for.)

locations._rule: Every new character adds exactly 3 locations to the world map.

home_base.name: Kettlebrook
home_base.from_worlds_middle: Heartland — institutional core, kingdom infrastructure everywhere
home_base.terrain_and_vibe: A modest coalition town a half-day's walk from Varenholm. Brick row houses, three inns, a small market square, a temple to the common gods. Halfling and human population mixed. Working-class — the people who wash the sheets and shoe the horses for the capital.
home_base.travel_time_to_major_hub: Half a day on foot to Varenholm, 2 hours by cart
home_base.connection_to_character: Cookie grew up here. She and her mother Daisy clean rooms at The Wren's Rest inn. Cookie knows every floorboard, every regular, every shortcut through the back alleys. This is the place she loves and needs to leave.
home_base.registry_entry: Kettlebrook — working-class coalition town near Varenholm, halfling/human mixed population. Cookie's hometown.

adventure_site.name: The Sunken Playhouse
adventure_site.distance_from_home_base: 2 days travel (between Kettlebrook and Varenholm, off the main road)
adventure_site.terrain_and_vibe: An ancient outdoor amphitheater that sank into the earth during a ley-line shift decades ago. Half-buried stone seats, a cracked stage, acoustics that still carry whispers across the ruin. Now occupied by Lyssa and her troupe — a rival performance company that uses charm magic, theft, and intimidation to eliminate competition.
adventure_site.why_dangerous: Base of operations for Lyssa Vane, a charismatic bard-enchantress who controls the regional performance circuit through blackmail, sabotage, and magical coercion. Anyone who threatens her monopoly on heartland entertainment gets silenced.
adventure_site.registry_entry: The Sunken Playhouse — collapsed pre-kingdom amphitheater between Kettlebrook and Varenholm. Occupied by a rogue bard troupe.

discovery_location.name: The Resonance Chamber
discovery_location.connection_to_existing_lore: Pre-kingdom ley-line infrastructure. The chamber sits on a junction where the amphitheater's acoustics connect to a deeper network — a place where sound becomes magic. Ancient bards used it. It also resonates with Ember energy, which is why Cookie feels drawn to it. A young dragon (connected to the dragon treaty edge case) has been nesting here, drawn by the same resonance.
discovery_location.what_the_player_learns_here: Cookie discovers that her dancing and singing produce visible magical effects in this chamber — golden light, warmth, healing. She realizes her talent isn't just talent. Something inside her is different. The chamber's carvings reference 'the children of fire' — an old term that predates the kingdom. A young dragon sheltering here recognizes Cookie's Ember spark and reacts to her as kin. Cookie begins to understand she's connected to something much bigger than bard school.
key_npcs_here: Young bronze dragon (unnamed — treaty edge case)
Echo (a spirit/memory trapped in the chamber's acoustics)
discovery_location.registry_entry: The Resonance Chamber — hidden ley-line acoustic junction beneath the Sunken Playhouse. Pre-kingdom bard infrastructure, Ember-resonant.

antagonist._rule: Scaled for levels 5-8. Regional menace, not world-ending.
antagonist.name: Lyssa Vane
antagonist.motivation: Lyssa was a talented bard who graduated from Varenholm Academy 10 years ago. She built a performance empire in the heartland through charm magic and intimidation. She controls which acts get booked, which venues stay open, and which performers get 'opportunities.' Anyone more talented than her gets absorbed into her troupe (charmed) or destroyed. Cookie — a natural talent with supernatural charisma and an Ember spark — is everything Lyssa fears: someone who could outshine her without even trying.
antagonist.relationship_to_goal: rival — Lyssa will not allow another bard to rise in the heartland without her permission. Cookie's raw talent and Ankuspawn magnetism threaten Lyssa's entire operation.

lieutenants.name: Rook
lieutenants.role: Enforcer. Former bouncer turned muscle. Handles the physical intimidation — wrecking instruments, threatening venue owners, roughing up performers who don't pay Lyssa's 'booking fee.'

lieutenants.name: Silka
lieutenants.role: Spy. A charmed performer planted at the Varenholm Academy as a student. Reports back to Lyssa about promising new talent — especially anyone with unusual abilities or gold eyes.

lieutenants.name: Eira
lieutenants.role: Recruiter. Approaches talented performers with 'opportunities' that are actually traps — join Lyssa's troupe willingly or get charmed into it. Operates near the discovery location.

antagonist.defeat_consequences: Lyssa's charm network collapses. The charmed performers in her troupe wake up and scatter — some grateful, some traumatized. The heartland performance circuit opens up. Venues that were paying protection money are free. Cookie's path to fame is clear. But Lyssa's fall reveals she had a patron — someone was paying her to watch for gold-eyed performers and report them. The patron's identity leads to the Cult of Anku (continuation hook).
antagonist.dm_secret: Lyssa doesn't know about the Cult of Anku directly. She was contacted by an anonymous patron 2 years ago who pays her 5 SP per month to report any performers with 'unusual golden eyes or supernatural charm.' She thinks it's a noble with a fetish. It's actually a Cult of Anku recruiter building a target list.

campaign_spine._rule: Goal achievable by level 5-6. Remainder is defending it from the antagonist.

phase_1_arrival.summary: Cookie leaves Kettlebrook on her 21st birthday. Breaks the news to her mom. Travels to Varenholm. Enrolls in bard school. Discovers she has natural talent that goes beyond practice — her dances literally glow. Makes friends and enemies. First brush with Lyssa's network when a venue cancels Cookie's first student performance.
key_scenes: Scene 1: Cookie's 21st birthday morning. The Wren's Rest kitchen. She tells Daisy she's leaving. Daisy's reaction — anger, fear, tears, 'no daughter of mine.' Cookie leaves with 5 GP and a packed bag.

Scene 2: Arrival at Varenholm Academy. Registration. Cookie pays 1 GP tuition. Meets her first classmates. Realizes she's the poorest student there — everyone else has instruments, fine clothes, connections.
Scene 3: First dance class. Cookie performs and the room goes quiet. Golden light traces her movements. The instructor is stunned. Cookie doesn't understand why everyone is staring.

Scene 4: Cookie's first booked performance at a tavern gets cancelled last minute. The owner is apologetic but scared. 'Someone told me not to book new acts without clearance.' First hint of Lyssa's network.
phase_1_arrival.goal_status: visible but obstructed — Cookie has talent but the system is rigged against newcomers

phase_2_achievement.summary: Cookie learns to control her abilities. Her performances draw crowds. She earns a reputation — 'the golden dancer.' She performs at a major festival and gets noticed kingdom-wide. She's becoming what she dreamed of. Then Lyssa notices.
key_scenes: Cookie masters her first real performance — a dance that heals the audience. Word spreads.
Cookie performs at a Varenholm harvest festival. The crowd is massive. For one night, she feels like Ignis.
The next morning, her practice room at the Academy has been trashed. Instruments broken. A note: 'The heartland has enough bards.'
phase_2_achievement.goal_status: achieved — Cookie is becoming famous. Now someone wants to stop her.

phase_3_defense.summary: Lyssa escalates. Cookie's performances are sabotaged. A friend is charmed and turned against her. Cookie discovers the Sunken Playhouse and Lyssa's operation. She also finds the Resonance Chamber and begins to understand what she really is. The young dragon in the chamber recognizes her Ember spark.
key_scenes: Fern (Cookie's best friend from Kettlebrook, who followed her to Varenholm) goes missing. Cookie tracks her to Lyssa's troupe — Fern has been charmed.

Cookie finds the Resonance Chamber. Her dancing in that space produces full magical effects — healing, light, warmth. The carvings mention 'children of fire.' A young bronze dragon nesting there doesn't attack — it watches, fascinated.
Lyssa sends Rook to deliver a final warning. Cookie has to fight or talk her way out. Either way, she realizes Lyssa won't stop until one of them is gone.
phase_3_defense.goal_status: threatened — Cookie's reputation, friends, and safety are all under attack

phase_4_confrontation.summary: Cookie assaults the Sunken Playhouse to free the charmed performers and end Lyssa's monopoly. The final confrontation is a performance duel — bard vs. bard, charm vs. genuine talent. Cookie wins, frees the troupe, and secures her place in the heartland music scene. The Resonance Chamber reveals its deeper secret — and Lyssa's anonymous patron points toward a much larger threat.
key_scenes: Cookie enters the Sunken Playhouse. The acoustics amplify everything — this is Lyssa's home turf. Rook and the charmed troupe stand between Cookie and Lyssa.
The duel: Lyssa uses Glamour magic to dominate the stage. Cookie uses her Ember-powered dancing — genuine, raw, golden. The charmed performers start to wake up.

Lyssa falls. The charm breaks. Fern is free. The Sunken Playhouse is reclaimed. Cookie finds Lyssa's ledger — payments from an anonymous patron asking about 'gold-eyed performers.' The Cult of Anku thread opens.

Epilogue: Cookie performs at the reclaimed Playhouse. The bronze dragon from the Resonance Chamber watches from the treeline. Cookie doesn't know what she is yet — but she knows she's more than just a dancer.
phase_4_confrontation.goal_status: secured — Cookie is a famous bard. The road ahead leads to bigger questions.

main_cast.name: Daisy
main_cast.want: Wants Cookie safe, close, and away from anything that draws attention. Terrified of what Cookie is without understanding it.
main_cast.voice_hook: Talks in clipped sentences when angry, long rambling ones when worried. Calls Cookie 'little bean' when she forgets to be mad.
main_cast.lore_connection: Met Kenji once, 22 years ago, at an inn she was cleaning. He was passing through. She never learned his real name. She knows Cookie's father was 'someone important' because of the way her body changed after — she can't have more children and doesn't know why. She blames herself, not him.

main_cast.name: Tomas Wren
main_cast.want: Wants his inn to stay profitable and his employees to stop causing drama. Secretly proud of Cookie and slips her performance tips when Daisy isn't looking.
main_cast.voice_hook: Dry, understated. 'That's nice, dear. Now mop the landing.' But his eyes crinkle when Cookie dances.
main_cast.lore_connection: Served in the coalition army 20 years ago. Saw Varenholm during the founding. Retired to run an inn. Knows more about the world than he lets on. Has seen gold eyes before — on a traveler, years ago — and said nothing.

main_cast.name: Fern
main_cast.want: Wants adventure. Wants to be Cookie's best friend forever. Wants to not be boring. Will do reckless things for fun.
main_cast.voice_hook: Talks too fast, laughs too loud, asks questions she shouldn't. 'Wait wait wait — you're telling me that guy just GAVE you flowers? For dancing? Can you teach me that?'
main_cast.lore_connection: Human girl from Kettlebrook. No special heritage. Just Cookie's ride-or-die since they were kids. Gets charmed by Lyssa in Phase 3 — the emotional core of the campaign.

main_cast.name: Professor Cadence Ashworth
main_cast.want: Wants to understand Cookie's abilities. Has never seen a student produce visible magic through dance alone without formal training. Suspects Cookie is 'touched by something' but doesn't know what.
main_cast.voice_hook: Measured, precise. Every word chosen carefully. 'Again. Slower. Watch where the light starts — it's not in your hands, it's in your spine. Do you feel that?'
main_cast.lore_connection: Studied at the Academy 30 years ago. Overlapped briefly with early coalition era. Has heard rumors of 'the Emperor's children' but considers it tavern gossip. Will revise that opinion when Cookie's abilities develop.

main_cast.name: Lyssa Vane
main_cast.want: Total control of heartland entertainment. No one shines brighter than her. She worked for everything she has and she'll burn anyone who threatens it — especially some barefoot halfling from Kettlebrook who glows when she dances.
main_cast.voice_hook: Silky, warm, complimentary — until the mask drops. 'You're so talented, darling. It would be such a shame if something happened to that voice.' Switches from charm to venom mid-sentence.
main_cast.lore_connection: Graduated Varenholm Academy. Built her empire legitimately at first, then turned to Glamour magic when competition got fierce. Has an anonymous patron paying her to report gold-eyed performers — doesn't know it's the Cult of Anku.

dialogue.intro_scene: Morning. The Wren's Rest kitchen. Cookie is 21 today. The birthday bread her mother baked sits on the counter, still warm. Daisy is scrubbing a pot and humming — she always hums when she's nervous, like she can feel something coming. Cookie's packed bag is hidden under her bed upstairs. The 5 gold pieces are sewn into the lining of her cloak. Today she has to say it.
rumor_mill_examples: 'Did you hear about the girl from Millford? Gold eyes. Went to study at the Ashenveil. Never wrote home.'
'The Firebird is performing in Varenholm next month. Ignis. They say she breathes real fire on stage and the crowd goes mad.'
'Don't book at the Copper Bell — Lyssa Vane's people told them no new acts. You want to perform in this town, you go through her.'
'There's something under the old playhouse ruin. Shepherds hear music at night. No one goes near it.'
dialogue.antagonist_confrontation_line: Lyssa leans close, perfume and venom: 'You think you're special because you glow? I've seen a hundred girls like you. They all burned out. The only question is whether you burn out quietly or I help.'
dialogue.discovery_reveal_moment: Cookie dances alone in the Resonance Chamber. The stone floor warms under her feet. Golden light spirals from her fingertips, her spine, her breath. The carvings on the walls illuminate — 'the children of fire shall carry the spark beyond the age of the flame.' A bronze dragon the size of a horse lifts its head from the shadows. It doesn't roar. It hums — the same note Cookie was singing. They are resonating. She is not just a dancer. She is something else entirely.
dialogue.peaceful_outro_hint: The Sunken Playhouse has been cleared. Kettlebrook can hear the music from the road on quiet nights. Cookie performs to a full house — golden light, warm air, tears in the audience. Daisy is in the back row, arms crossed, crying. Fern is in the front row, cheering too loud. The bronze dragon watches from the treeline, unseen. And somewhere in the southeast, a woman in a dark academy reads a name on a list: 'Cookie. Halfling. Gold eyes. Kettlebrook. Confirmed Ankuspawn.' The next campaign begins.

success_state: Cookie is a famous bard in the heartland. Lyssa Vane is defeated. The charmed performers are free. The Sunken Playhouse is reclaimed as a legitimate venue. The Resonance Chamber is discovered and its Ember connection begins Cookie's deeper journey. Fern is safe. Daisy is proud (grudgingly). The Cult of Anku thread is open — Cookie is now visible to people who hunt what she is.
failure_state: Lyssa charms or destroys Cookie's reputation. Fern remains charmed. Cookie is driven out of Varenholm and returns to Kettlebrook — a failed bard, exactly what her mother predicted. The Sunken Playhouse remains Lyssa's stronghold. The Resonance Chamber stays hidden. Cookie never learns what she is. Worse: the Cult of Anku finds her anyway, and without allies or power, she has no defense.

post_campaign._note: At level 10+, snapshot this file, update realm_lore_registry.json, and the next new character inherits everything.
continuation_hooks: Cult of Anku — Lyssa's anonymous patron is a Cult recruiter. Cookie is now on their list. The next arc involves the Ashenveil Academy and Nyx's harvesting operation.
The bronze dragon — who is it? Why is it in the heartland? Is it violating the dragon treaty? Does it know Cookie's father?
Cookie's heritage — she now knows she has Ember magic. She doesn't know what Ankuspawn are, who her father is, or why gold-eyed people keep disappearing. The search for answers leads to Kenji.
Ignis — Cookie's idol. They share a father. If they ever meet, the resonance between their Ember sparks would be unmistakable. Ignis might be the first person to tell Cookie the truth.

mechanical_state._merge_note: StoryEngine preserves unknown top-level keys on save.
mechanical_state.starting_gold: 5 GP (life savings — tuition 1 GP/year, 4 GP remaining after year 1)

core: Individual Ember spark (manifests as golden light when dancing/singing)
female: High magical aptitude across all domains
Valuable biological resources (target for Cult of Anku)
ankuspawn_traits.halfling_variable: +1 Charisma (chosen). ~4'10" height. Halfling Luck (reroll natural 1s). Light-footed and nimble. Disarmingly charming — people underestimate her constantly.
